title: ilex colchica subsp. colchica
intro: Ilex colchica subsp. colchica, also known as the Caucasian holly or Caucasian alder, is a subspecies of evergreen shrub native to the mountainous regions of the Caucasus. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and improve digestion, due to the presence of compounds such as alkaloids and flavonoids. The health benefits of Ilex colchica subsp. colchica include its potential to help lower blood pressure, improve immune function, and act as an antioxidant. Ilex colchica subsp. colchica is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and can also be found in herbal supplements and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
